title: anaphrenium verticillatum
intro: Anaphrenium verticillatum, also known as 'whorled anaphrenium' or 'whorled puccoon', is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its yellow flowers that grow in whorls or clusters around the stem. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and fever, as well as being used as a treatment for rheumatism and other inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, the roots of Anaphrenium verticillatum are typically dried and then used in teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal benefits.
